Porifera > Demospongiae > Suberitida > > Halichondriidae

Crumbly sponge

Amorphinopsis excavans*

Updated Oct 2016

Where seen?

This colourful sponge with a crumbly-looking texture is sometimes seen on our Northern shores. Often seen near Rainbow sponges ( Suberites sp.)

Features

Encrusting to irregular boulder-shaped sponge 10-15cm, surface rough and irregular. Holes not obvious out of water. On the outside, greyish-green, blue. Those growing in shade said to be yellowish. Bright yellow on the inside.

Punggol, Aug 13

Berlayar Creek, Dec 10 Photo shared by Loh Kok Sheng on flickr .

**Species are difficult to positively identify without close examination.

On this website, they are grouped by external features for convenience of display.*

Crumbly sponges on Singapore shores

Photos of Crumbly sponges for free download from wildsingapore flickr

Distribution in Singapore on this wildsingapore flickr map

References

  • Swee-Cheng Lim, Nicole J. de Voogd and Koh-Siang Tan. Fouling sponges (Porifera) on navigation buoys from Singapore waters . The Raffles Bulletin of Zoology Supplement No. 22: 41-68, 30 Dec 2009 Pp. 41-58.
  • Lim Swee Cheng, Nicole de Voogd and Tan Koh Siang. 2008. A Guide to Sponges of Singapore . Singapore Science Centre. 173pp.
